TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @misc{Wiki_szy_nipaluma-sumanahay_kayabes,_somanahay_kayabes, title = nipaluma-sumanahay_kayabes,_somanahay_kayabes, year = 2025, month = May, day = 5, note = {In Wikipedia [Sakizaya]}, url = {https://szy.wikipedia.org/wiki/nipaluma-sumanahay_kayabes,_somanahay_kayabes} }

S.FORM: == sumanahay kayabes, somanahay kayabes == 紅心芭樂 kagak kakuniza subal :真核域 Eukaryota salaedan :植物界 Plantae panan :真雙子葉植物 Eudicots upiz : mata :桃金孃目 Myrtales sapamat :桃金孃科 Myrtaceae mikitinay :番石榴屬 Psidium hicahicaay :番石榴 P. guajava u siwkay nu nipaluma u sanek nu nipaluma malaheci tu imelang pasubana i cacudadan a lacul u zuma a cidekay a kamu, saungay misapayu' a cidekay u sulit nu zuma a kamu-Hulam atu Amilika Psidium guajava, the common guava, yellow guava, or lemon guava (known as goiaba in Portuguese and guayaba in Spanish) is an evergreen shrub or small tree native to the Caribbean, Central America and South America. It is easily pollinated by insects; in culture, mainly by the common honey bee, Apis mellifera.
S.PHON: == sumanaħaj kajabəs, somanaħaj kajabəs == **** kagak kakuniza subar~ɾ :*** əukarjota sar~ɾaədan :*** pr~ɾantaə panan :****** əudiʦots upiz : mata :**** mjrtar~ɾəs sapamat :**** mjrtaʦəaə mikitinaj :**** psidium ħiʦaħiʦaaj :*** p. guaja*a u siwkaj nu nipar~ɾuma u sanək nu nipar~ɾuma mar~ɾaħəʦi tu imər~ɾaŋ pasubana i ʦaʦudadan a r~ɾaʦur~ɾ u zuma a ʦidəkaj a kamu, sauŋaj misapajuʔ~ʡ a ʦidəkaj u sur~ɾit nu zuma a kamu-ħur~ɾam atu amir~ɾika psidium guaja*a, tħə ʦommon gua*a, jər~ɾr~ɾow gua*a, or r~ɾəmon gua*a (known as goiaba in portuguəsə and guajaba in spanisħ) is an ə*ərgrəən sħrub or smar~ɾr~ɾ trəə nati*ə to tħə ʦaribbəan, ʦəntrar~ɾ aməriʦa and soutħ aməriʦa. it is əasir~ɾj por~ɾr~ɾinatəd bj insəʦts; in ʦur~ɾturə, mainr~ɾj bj tħə ʦommon ħonə bəə, apis mər~ɾr~ɾifəra.
S.FORM: == sumanahay kayabes, somanahay kayabes == 紅心芭樂 kagak kakuniza subal :真核域 Eukaryota salaedan :植物界 Plantae panan :真雙子葉植物 Eudicots upiz : mata :桃金孃目 Myrtales sapamat :桃金孃科 Myrtaceae mikitinay :番石榴屬 Psidium hicahicaay :番石榴 P. guajava u siwkay nu nipaluma u sanek nu nipaluma malaheci tu imelang pasubana i cacudadan a lacul u zuma a cidekay a kamu, saungay misapayu' a cidekay u sulit nu zuma a kamu-Hulam atu Amilika Psidium guajava, the common guava, yellow guava, or lemon guava (known as goiaba in Portuguese and guayaba in Spanish) is an evergreen shrub or small tree native to the Caribbean, Central America and South America. It is easily pollinated by insects; in culture, mainly by the common honey bee, Apis mellifera.
S.PHON: == sumanaħaj kajabəs, somanaħaj kajabəs == **** kagak kakuniza subar~ɾ :*** əukarjota sar~ɾaədan :*** pr~ɾantaə panan :****** əudiʦots upiz : mata :**** mjrtar~ɾəs sapamat :**** mjrtaʦəaə mikitinaj :**** psidium ħiʦaħiʦaaj :*** p. guaja*a u siwkaj nu nipar~ɾuma u sanək nu nipar~ɾuma mar~ɾaħəʦi tu imər~ɾaŋ pasubana i ʦaʦudadan a r~ɾaʦur~ɾ u zuma a ʦidəkaj a kamu, sauŋaj misapajuʔ~ʡ a ʦidəkaj u sur~ɾit nu zuma a kamu-ħur~ɾam atu amir~ɾika psidium guaja*a, tħə ʦommon gua*a, jər~ɾr~ɾow gua*a, or r~ɾəmon gua*a (known as goiaba in portuguəsə and guajaba in spanisħ) is an ə*ərgrəən sħrub or smar~ɾr~ɾ trəə nati*ə to tħə ʦaribbəan, ʦəntrar~ɾ aməriʦa and soutħ aməriʦa. it is əasir~ɾj por~ɾr~ɾinatəd bj insəʦts; in ʦur~ɾturə, mainr~ɾj bj tħə ʦommon ħonə bəə, apis mər~ɾr~ɾifəra.
